scope: Inventory the current enterprise posture after M19 and land the first shared M20 hardening artifact without forking the native M16/M18 contract.

first_shared_hardening_artifact.name: runtime_worker_capability_catalog
purpose: make worker capabilities explicit instead of inferred from client-local logic
keep chat, vs_code, cli, workflow, and chatops on one native worker or session contract
give later M20 policy packs one authoritative capability inventory to target

multi_tenant_policy_packs: reusable enterprise policy bundles by deployment mode
policy-pack attachment rules for clients and workers
rbac_refinement: enterprise operator role bundles across UI, IDE, CLI, workflow, and chatops
clear separation between review, decision, and execution roles
dlp_and_secret_policy_enforcement: outbound deny or escalation rules beyond the current governed report redaction pass

runtime-native export-time secret scanning and policy hooks for the remaining incident export path and any future server-side governed export routes beyond the now-covered run, audit, and evidence exports
credential-class policy hooks without client-specific branching
worker_capability_matrices: capability grant classes mapped to worker categories and environments
verifier coverage for capability policy drift
reporting_and_export_integration: downstream export profiles and sink mappings for SIEM, GRC, and ticket systems beyond the current shared workflow/chatops/CLI/Chat/VS Code report envelope
stronger contract between audit/evidence exports and external governance sinks
server-side governed export routes beyond the current shared run, audit, and evidence export coverage
enterprise_it_admin_and_org_scale_operat: runtime org-admin inventory now covers centralized enterprise, federated business-unit, and regulated regional operating models
delegated admin and break-glass role bundles are cataloged on the same native contract as worker-capability, policy-pack, and export-profile catalogs
group-to-role mapping inputs for directory-sync or identity-claim driven scope attachment are now inventoried
residency, legal hold, network boundary, fleet rollout, quota, and chargeback dimensions are now durable runtime inventory
desktop Chat and VS Code governed reports now consume the org-admin inventory on the shared enterprise report envelope without introducing a second admin-report contract
structured org-admin enforcement profiles now project delegated-admin, break-glass, directory-sync, residency, legal-hold, quota, and chargeback hooks into the shared governed report contract

structured directory-sync mappings, exception profiles, and overlay profiles now project into the shared Go, desktop Chat, and VS Code governed report and export surfaces without introducing a second admin/export contract

structured decision bindings now project delegated-admin, break-glass, directory-sync, residency/legal-hold exception, and quota/chargeback overlay decisions into the shared Go, desktop Chat, VS Code, and desktop governed export surfaces without introducing a second admin/export contract

delegated-admin decision bindings now persist through native approval checkpoints and project active org-admin review state across shared Go, desktop Chat, VS Code, CLI, workflow, and chatops governed report surfaces on the same contract

structured decision bindings, overlays, exceptions, and normalized input values are now cataloged, persisted on the native approval path, and projected into shared governed review surfaces, but broader delegated-admin and break-glass admin-surface enforcement, SCIM ingestion, residency/legal-hold enforcement, and org-level quota or chargeback implementation are still required

shared Go, desktop Chat, and VS Code governed report surfaces now render persisted org-admin input values and category-specific action hints directly from approval annotations on the same native contract

next_batch: Extend runtime-side enforcement and persistence from the current approval-checkpoint org-admin binding path into the broader delegated-admin, break-glass, directory-sync, residency/legal-hold exception, and quota/chargeback overlay admin surfaces.
Extend org-admin binding, exception, overlay, and normalized input-value metadata into the remaining governed report and export surfaces without forking the native M16/M18 contract.
Extend parity and verifier coverage for structured org-admin binding and input-state metadata across desktop Chat, VS Code, CLI, workflow, and chatops.
Keep Chat, VS Code, CLI, workflow, and chatops on the same native M16/M18 contract.
Do not introduce client-specific hardening branches.

second_shared_hardening_artifact.name: runtime_policy_pack_catalog
purpose: make the first reusable role-and-capability hardening packs explicit at runtime
keep policy-pack attachment rules on the same native worker and execution contract used by all ingress surfaces
provide one authoritative policy-pack inventory for later RBAC and capability-matrix refinement

third_shared_hardening_artifact.name: runtime_export_profile_catalog
purpose: make governed export-profile choices explicit instead of inferred from client-local defaults
keep report and export review surfaces on the same native contract while exposing audience, retention, delivery, and redaction policy
provide one authoritative export-profile inventory for later retention-class, audience-selection, and downstream-governance integration work

fourth_shared_hardening_artifact.name: runtime_org_admin_governed_report_projection
purpose: project org-admin inventory into governed report surfaces without creating a second admin-report contract
surface delegated-admin, break-glass, directory-sync, residency, legal-hold, network, fleet, quota, chargeback, and enforcement-hook metadata on the same shared report envelope
keep enterprise IT/admin inventory attached to the native M16/M18 contract rather than inventing client-specific hardening branches

fifth_shared_hardening_artifact.name: runtime_org_admin_enforcement_profiles
purpose: carry delegated-admin, break-glass, directory-sync, residency, legal-hold, quota, and chargeback enforcement profiles on the same native org-admin catalog
project role bundles, required inputs, decision surfaces, and boundary requirements into shared governed report surfaces
prepare later runtime enforcement without inventing a second admin or export contract
